changes.value:
## What
Add a `mode` prop to `CommandPalette` ("command" | "search"). In search mode, the palette fuzzy-matches all entities from `EntityStore` instead of filtering commands. Results show entity type badge + display name. Selection dispatches `entity.inspect` via `useInspect()` context.

**Files:** `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx`

**Approach:**
- Add `mode` prop defaulting to "command"
- In search mode, use `useEntityStore().getEntities()` across all known types (task, tag, column, actor, swimlane)
- Fuzzy match query against entity display fields using existing `fuzzyMatch()` 
- Show entity type as a muted prefix, entity display name as main text
- Debounce search input (150ms) for smooth typing
- On Enter/click, call `useInspect()` with the entity moniker (`type:id`)
- Placeholder text changes to "Search entities..."

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search mode shows all entities when filter is empty (or top N)
- [ ] Typing fuzzy-filters across entity names/fields
- [ ] Selecting a result opens its inspector panel
- [ ] Escape closes search palette (same as command mode)
- [ ] Vim/CUA/emacs keymap modes all work in search input

## Tests
- [ ] Existing command palette tests still pass
- [ ] Manual: open search mode, type query, results filter, select opens inspector

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,22 +1,22 @@
 ## What
-Add a `mode` prop to `CommandPalette` ("command" | "search"). In search mode, the palette fuzzy-matches all entities from `EntityStore` instead of filtering commands. Results show entity type badge + display name. Selection dispatches `entity.inspect` via `useInspect()` context.
+Add a `mode` prop to `CommandPalette` ("command" | "search"). In search mode, the palette calls the Rust-side `search_entities` Tauri command (which uses EntitySearchIndex) instead of filtering commands. Results show entity type badge + display name (using `search_display_field`). Selection dispatches inspect via `useInspect()`.

 **Files:** `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx`

 **Approach:**
 - Add `mode` prop defaulting to "command"
-- In search mode, use `useEntityStore().getEntities()` across all known types (task, tag, column, actor, swimlane)
-- Fuzzy match query against entity display fields using existing `fuzzyMatch()` 
-- Show entity type as a muted prefix, entity display name as main text
-- Debounce search input (150ms) for smooth typing
-- On Enter/click, call `useInspect()` with the entity moniker (`type:id`)
-- Placeholder text changes to "Search entities..."
+- In search mode, debounce input (150ms) and call `invoke("search_entities", { query, limit: 50 })`
+- Results come back as `[{ entity_type, entity_id, display_name, score }]`
+- Show entity type as a muted prefix, display_name as main text
+- On Enter/click, call `useInspect()` with the entity moniker (`type:id`) — uses command dispatch, not hardcoded
+- Placeholder text changes to "Search..."

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Search mode shows all entities when filter is empty (or top N)
-- [ ] Typing fuzzy-filters across entity names/fields
-- [ ] Selecting a result opens its inspector panel
-- [ ] Escape closes search palette (same as command mode)
+- [ ] Search mode calls Rust-side search (not client-side fuzzy)
+- [ ] Debounced input (150ms) prevents excessive Tauri calls
+- [ ] Results show entity type + display name (no field captions)
+- [ ] Selecting a result opens its inspector panel via command dispatch
+- [ ] Escape closes search palette
 - [ ] Vim/CUA/emacs keymap modes all work in search input

 ## Tests

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,5 +1,13 @@
 ## What
-Add a `mode` prop to `CommandPalette` ("command" | "search"). In search mode, the palette calls the Rust-side `search_entities` Tauri command (which uses EntitySearchIndex) instead of filtering commands. Results show entity type badge + display name (using `search_display_field`). Selection dispatches inspect via `useInspect()`.
+Add a `mode` prop to `CommandPalette` ("command" | "search"). In search mode, the palette calls `invoke("search_entities", { query, limit })` (the Rust-side Tauri command backed by EntitySearchIndex) and displays results with entity type + display name.
+
+On selection, calls `useInspect(moniker)` — this is the same pattern every component uses (`entity-card.tsx`, `tag-pill.tsx`, `focus-scope.tsx` etc.). Their `entity.inspect` execute handlers all call `useInspect()` under the hood. This is NOT hardcoding — it IS the architectural pattern for opening inspectors.
+
+**Architecture note on inspect dispatch:**
+- `entity.inspect` is a frontend-scoped command registered per-FocusScope — requires a FocusScope wrapping the element
+- `ui.inspect` is a Rust command that mutates UIState but the frontend doesn't react to UIState changes for rendering — it drives its own `panelStack` via React state
+- `useInspect()` from `inspect-context.tsx` is the shared mechanism that all inspect paths converge on
+- From the search palette (no FocusScope), `useInspect(moniker)` is the correct call

 **Files:** `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx`

@@ -7,18 +15,19 @@
 - Add `mode` prop defaulting to "command"
 - In search mode, debounce input (150ms) and call `invoke("search_entities", { query, limit: 50 })`
 - Results come back as `[{ entity_type, entity_id, display_name, score }]`
-- Show entity type as a muted prefix, display_name as main text
-- On Enter/click, call `useInspect()` with the entity moniker (`type:id`) — uses command dispatch, not hardcoded
+- Show entity type as a muted prefix, display_name as main text (no field captions)
+- On Enter/click, call `inspect("entity_type:entity_id")` via `useInspect()`
 - Placeholder text changes to "Search..."
+- Empty query shows nothing (or a "type to search" hint)

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Search mode calls Rust-side search (not client-side fuzzy)
-- [ ] Debounced input (150ms) prevents excessive Tauri calls
-- [ ] Results show entity type + display name (no field captions)
-- [ ] Selecting a result opens its inspector panel via command dispatch
+- [ ] Search mode calls Rust-side search via Tauri invoke
+- [ ] Debounced input (150ms) prevents excessive calls
+- [ ] Results show entity type + display name
+- [ ] Selecting a result opens inspector via useInspect()
 - [ ] Escape closes search palette
-- [ ] Vim/CUA/emacs keymap modes all work in search input
+- [ ] Vim/CUA/emacs keymap modes work in search input

 ## Tests
 - [ ] Existing command palette tests still pass
-- [ ] Manual: open search mode, type query, results filter, select opens inspector
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] Manual: search, select result, inspector opens
\ No newline at end of file

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,13 +1,7 @@
 ## What
 Add a `mode` prop to `CommandPalette` ("command" | "search"). In search mode, the palette calls `invoke("search_entities", { query, limit })` (the Rust-side Tauri command backed by EntitySearchIndex) and displays results with entity type + display name.

-On selection, calls `useInspect(moniker)` — this is the same pattern every component uses (`entity-card.tsx`, `tag-pill.tsx`, `focus-scope.tsx` etc.). Their `entity.inspect` execute handlers all call `useInspect()` under the hood. This is NOT hardcoding — it IS the architectural pattern for opening inspectors.
-
-**Architecture note on inspect dispatch:**
-- `entity.inspect` is a frontend-scoped command registered per-FocusScope — requires a FocusScope wrapping the element
-- `ui.inspect` is a Rust command that mutates UIState but the frontend doesn't react to UIState changes for rendering — it drives its own `panelStack` via React state
-- `useInspect()` from `inspect-context.tsx` is the shared mechanism that all inspect paths converge on
-- From the search palette (no FocusScope), `useInspect(moniker)` is the correct call
+Each search result row is wrapped in a `FocusScope` with an `entity.inspect` command registered — the same pattern used by `entity-card.tsx`, `tag-pill.tsx`, `column-view.tsx`, etc. Selecting a result sets focus and dispatches `entity.inspect` through the scope chain. This means search results are first-class entities in the command system: they get focus, context menus, and inspect all works through normal dispatch.

 **Files:** `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx`

@@ -15,19 +9,22 @@
 - Add `mode` prop defaulting to "command"
 - In search mode, debounce input (150ms) and call `invoke("search_entities", { query, limit: 50 })`
 - Results come back as `[{ entity_type, entity_id, display_name, score }]`
+- Each result row is a `FocusScope` with moniker `"type:id"` and commands `[{ id: "entity.inspect", execute: () => inspect(moniker) }]`
 - Show entity type as a muted prefix, display_name as main text (no field captions)
-- On Enter/click, call `inspect("entity_type:entity_id")` via `useInspect()`
+- On Enter or click, the FocusScope's `entity.inspect` fires through the scope chain
+- Arrow keys / j/k change selection (and could set focus to that result's FocusScope)
 - Placeholder text changes to "Search..."
 - Empty query shows nothing (or a "type to search" hint)

 ## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Search results are wrapped in FocusScope (participate in command system)
+- [ ] Selecting a result dispatches entity.inspect through scope chain
 - [ ] Search mode calls Rust-side search via Tauri invoke
 - [ ] Debounced input (150ms) prevents excessive calls
 - [ ] Results show entity type + display name
-- [ ] Selecting a result opens inspector via useInspect()
 - [ ] Escape closes search palette
 - [ ] Vim/CUA/emacs keymap modes work in search input

 ## Tests
 - [ ] Existing command palette tests still pass
-- [ ] Manual: search, select result, inspector opens
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] Manual: search, select result, inspector opens via entity.inspect dispatch
\ No newline at end of file
changes.reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,8 +1,14 @@
 ## What
 Add a `mode` prop to `CommandPalette` ("command" | "search"). In search mode, the palette calls `invoke("search_entities", { query, limit })` (the Rust-side Tauri command backed by EntitySearchIndex) and displays results with entity type + display name.

-Each search result row is wrapped in a `FocusScope` with an `entity.inspect` command registered — the same pattern used by `entity-card.tsx`, `tag-pill.tsx`, `column-view.tsx`, etc. Selecting a result sets focus and dispatches `entity.inspect` through the scope chain. This means search results are first-class entities in the command system: they get focus, context menus, and inspect all works through normal dispatch.
+On selection, calls `useInspect(moniker)` — this is the same pattern every component uses (`entity-card.tsx`, `tag-pill.tsx`, `focus-scope.tsx` etc.). Their `entity.inspect` execute handlers all call `useInspect()` under the hood. This is NOT hardcoding — it IS the architectural pattern for opening inspectors.

+**Architecture note on inspect dispatch:**
+- `entity.inspect` is a frontend-scoped command registered per-FocusScope — requires a FocusScope wrapping the element
+- `ui.inspect` is a Rust command that mutates UIState but the frontend doesn't react to UIState changes for rendering — it drives its own `panelStack` via React state
+- `useInspect()` from `inspect-context.tsx` is the shared mechanism that all inspect paths converge on
+- From the search palette (no FocusScope), `useInspect(moniker)` is the correct call
+
 **Files:** `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx`

 **Approach:**
@@ -9,22 +15,19 @@
 - Add `mode` prop defaulting to "command"
 - In search mode, debounce input (150ms) and call `invoke("search_entities", { query, limit: 50 })`
 - Results come back as `[{ entity_type, entity_id, display_name, score }]`
-- Each result row is a `FocusScope` with moniker `"type:id"` and commands `[{ id: "entity.inspect", execute: () => inspect(moniker) }]`
 - Show entity type as a muted prefix, display_name as main text (no field captions)
-- On Enter or click, the FocusScope's `entity.inspect` fires through the scope chain
-- Arrow keys / j/k change selection (and could set focus to that result's FocusScope)
+- On Enter/click, call `inspect("entity_type:entity_id")` via `useInspect()`
 - Placeholder text changes to "Search..."
 - Empty query shows nothing (or a "type to search" hint)

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Search results are wrapped in FocusScope (participate in command system)
-- [ ] Selecting a result dispatches entity.inspect through scope chain
 - [ ] Search mode calls Rust-side search via Tauri invoke
 - [ ] Debounced input (150ms) prevents excessive calls
 - [ ] Results show entity type + display name
+- [ ] Selecting a result opens inspector via useInspect()
 - [ ] Escape closes search palette
 - [ ] Vim/CUA/emacs keymap modes work in search input

 ## Tests
 - [ ] Existing command palette tests still pass
-- [ ] Manual: search, select result, inspector opens via entity.inspect dispatch
